summary: Round 4 remains correct and honestly scoped. The new public seam labels the sole implementation as a non-deployable trusted-dealer simulation, keeps every descriptive randomness model fail-closed, and explicitly states that deployment gating is not yet wired and is necessary but insufficient. The tests exercise the real ShamirDealer paths for model reporting, nonzero-mask reconstruction, input reconstruction, and refusal of both the dealer and a self-labelled PRSS stub. The design and public documentation consistently avoid claiming that dealer-less cryptography, malicious nonzero enforcement, production integration, or external audit has landed. No new correctness, soundness, test-validity, or security issue is evident relative to the approved prior round.
